entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Career coach providing philosophy on personal branding and positioning

entities.name: Jake Gates
entities.context: Person receiving career coaching advice, struggling with 'why you' positioning

entities.name: Play Your Own Game
entities.context: V's framework for career positioning - focus on clarity about who you are rather than uniqueness

entities.name: Why You Framing
entities.context: Common career advice approach that V critiques as 'unintentionally unhelpful'

entities.name: Aggregate Uniqueness
entities.context: Belief that individual traits aren't unique but their combination is - 'patchwork of values and qualities'

entities.name: Non-Conventional Background Advantage
entities.context: Strategic principle that atypical backgrounds can be differentiators in competitive fields like consulting

entities.name: McKinsey
entities.context: Consulting firm referenced as example of conventional candidate expectations

entities.name: Sports Analogy
entities.context: Teaching technique - best teams play their own game rather than counter opponents

entities.name: Singapore Business Venture
entities.context: V's experience setting up a business in Singapore at age 24, used as example of compelling non-conventional experience

relationships.context: Career coaching on personal branding and positioning strategy
Core framework V advocates over 'what makes you unique' approach
Foundational belief that uniqueness comes from combination not individual traits
Personal experience proving atypical backgrounds can outcompete conventional ones
Teaching tool to explain Play Your Own Game concept
Alternative framework that reframes the positioning question
Consulting firms like McKinsey used as context for conventional vs non-conventional profiles
V's personal example demonstrating the principle
